summary: Key facts for 2026 Japan general election Feb 8: LDP had 196 seats pre-dissolution, needs 233 for majority. Major polls (Yomiuri, Nikkei Jan 27-28) indicate LDP on track for 233+ alone. Takaichi has ~70% approval. Coalition partner is now Nippon Ishin (not Komeito). Komeito joined CDP in opposition alliance.
content:
Election date: February 8, 2026. 465 seats (289 single-seat, 176 PR). PM Takaichi dissolved House Jan 23.

Pre-dissolution: LDP 196, Nippon Ishin 34 = 230 coalition seats.

Polling (Jan 24-25): LDP vote share 27-42%, most polls 35-39%.

Seat projections (Yomiuri/Nikkei Jan 27-28): LDP projected to exceed 233 alone. 'Stable majority' (243) 'within range'. Coalition could reach 'absolute stable majority' (261).

Key factors:
- Takaichi ~70% cabinet approval
- Komeito defected to opposition (CDP+Komeito 'Centrist Reform Alliance')
- But uncertainty whether cabinet popularity translates to LDP votes
- 2024 election: polls overestimated LDP (got 191 vs expectations)
- LDP needs to gain ~37 seats from 196 to 233
